title: Jaishankar Calls Pakistan 'Dalaal Nation' — Diplomatic Row Erupts
detail: Indian External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar publicly referred to Pakistan as a 'dalaal' (broker) nation at an all-party parliamentary meeting in New Delhi, dismissing its role as a mediator in the US-Iran and broader West Asia crisis. Jaishankar stated India is not a 'broker nation like Pakistan,' arguing Pakistan has historically been 'used' by the US since 1981. Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif responded calling Jaishankar 'a hi-fi dalal,' while the Pakistan FO labeled the remarks 'undiplomatic and megaphone theatrics.' President Zardari's spokesman accused Jaishankar of 'diplomatic dementia.' Within India, opposition leaders including Rahul Gandhi and Shashi Tharoor criticized the government for allowing Pakistan to gain geopolitical relevance while India remained a 'mute spectator' in West Asia diplomacy.

title: US Annual Threat Assessment Flags Pakistan Ballistic Missile Program
detail: The US Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) released its 2026 Annual Threat Assessment, which flagged Pakistan alongside Russia, China, North Korea, and Iran as developing advanced ballistic missile systems. DNI Tulsi Gabbard, testifying before the Senate Intelligence Committee, stated Pakistan is 'researching and developing advanced missile delivery systems that potentially could include ICBMs with range capable of striking the Homeland.' The report explicitly stated 'India-Pakistan relations remain a risk for nuclear conflict' and credited President Trump's intervention with de-escalating the most recent India-Pakistan military exchange (May 2025). Strategic analysts noted Pakistan currently fields no operational ICBMs, suggesting US concerns may relate to future Shaheen series developments.

title: Pakistan FO Condemns Jama Masjid Closure, Andrabi Sentence, Calls for Release
detail: Pakistan's Foreign Office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi, at the weekly press briefing, condemned the continued closure of Srinagar's historic Jama Masjid for the seventh consecutive year since August 5, 2019, calling it 'a serious violation of religious freedoms and fundamental human rights.' The FO also strongly condemned the life imprisonment sentence handed to Kashmiri separatist leader Asiya Andrabi and demanded her immediate release, calling the verdict politically motivated. Pakistan reiterated its support for Kashmiri self-determination rights.
